2606.23687.title: Randomized YaRN Improves Length Generalization for Long-Context Reasoning
2606.23687.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) are typically pretrained on short sequences and then extended to work on longer sequences with additional training. However, such LLMs still struggle to further generalize to very long sequences. We propose Randomized YaRN, a training method that improves length generalization by combining YaRN-based positional extrapolation with randomized positional encoding and a length curriculum. During training on short context data, tokens are assigned YaRN positional encodings sampled from a larger position range, exposing the model to out-of-distribution positional representations even on short-context inputs. We evaluate Randomized YaRN on two challenging long-context reasoning benchmarks, BABILong and Multi-Round Coreference Resolution (MRCR). When training on data with <8K context, Randomized YaRN consistently improves reasoning performance on context lengths from 16K to 128K and outperforms standard fine-tuning, with the largest gains appearing at far out-of-distribution lengths. Our results suggest that progressively exposing models to OOD positional distributions provides an effective recipe for generalizable long-context reasoning.
2606.23687.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）通常在短序列上進行預訓練，然後通過額外的訓練擴展到更長的序列。然而，這些LLMs仍然難以進一步推廣到非常長的序列。我們提出了隨機化YaRN，一種通過將基於YaRN的位置外推與隨機化位置編碼和長度課程相結合來改善長度泛化的訓練方法。在短上下文數據的訓練過程中，標記被分配從更大位置範圍中抽樣的YaRN位置編碼，即使在短上下文輸入中也使模型接觸到分佈外的位置表示。我們在兩個具有挑戰性的長上下文推理基準上評估隨機化YaRN，BABILong和多輪共指解析（MRCR）。在訓練上下文小於8K的數據時，隨機化YaRN始終改善了從16K到128K的上下文長度的推理性能，並且超越了標準微調，最大的增益出現在遠離分佈的長度上。我們的結果表明，逐步讓模型接觸到OOD位置分佈提供了一種有效的配方，用於可泛化的長上下文推理。
2606.23687.author: Manas Mehta et.al.
2606.23687.authors: Manas Mehta, Fangcong Yin, Greg Durrett
